_note: Machine-readable projection of ../../PROFILE.md (the single source of truth). Edit PROFILE.md first, then mirror the change here and run `python3 build.py`. README.md is GENERATED — never hand-edit it. Adding a project = adding one entry below, with no code change (CLAUDE.md 铁律 B).

brand.name: Dim Sum Parlor
brand.gloss: dim = dimension, sum = reduce. Dim Sum = 點心 = summing along a dimension.
brand._show_in_readme: Owner removed the meme line and the brand footer from the README on 2026-07-11 (commit 2adbf81). The facts stay here because the 3D site still needs them — this flag only governs the README.

bio: B.S.E. in Data Science at the University of Michigan, Ann Arbor. Working across LLM-guided incentive design, genomic foundation models, and AI-guided environmental sensing. Previously Mechanical Engineering at Shanghai Jiao Tong University.

cards._note: Verified live 2026-07-11. github-readme-stats.vercel.app and github-profile-trophy are BOTH DEAD (DEPLOYMENT_PAUSED / 402) — do not use them. Re-verify before adding any new card.

contributions._note: Self-generated by tools/contrib.py from GitHub's GraphQL contributionsCollection, committed as SVGs and refreshed daily by .github/workflows/contributions.yml. No third-party service — the two card hosts we tried both died. Window is rolling: it always ends at the current month.

research.title: LLM for Incentive Design in Mobility Systems
research.hook: Can an LLM design the incentives that make shared mobility actually work?
research.detail: An LLM-guided workflow that generates and evaluates interpretable incentive policies for ride-hailing, transit, and shared vehicles — pairing learning-based methods with classical optimization.

research.title: EPCOT Foundation Model — TSS-to-Expression Data Pipeline
research.hook: Made a genomic foundation model 2–5× faster to train — and found a 50 kb bug that was quietly wrecking its interpretability.
research.detail: Pseudobulk pipeline from raw snMultiome (1 bp sparse ATAC → 600 kb HDF5 windows; CP10K-normalized 19,264-gene RNA vectors across 7 cell types). Five bit-equivalent training optimizations for a 2–5× speedup at identical numerics. Reset-head fine-tuning for small-data generalization. Located and fixed a ~50 kb TSS-bin offset.

research.title: AI-Guided Environmental Sensing & Adaptive Monitoring
research.hook: Where should you put the next sensor in Lake Michigan? Let uncertainty decide.
research.detail: Reproducible ingestion → reconstruction → uncertainty-quantification workflow for the sparse, seasonal Great Lakes, comparing random / grid / uncertainty-aware sensor-placement rules.

research.title: Applets for Data Analytics in Materials Science
research.hook: Turning materials-science analysis into something you can just... upload a CSV to.
research.detail: JavaScript front end + Python back end serving the materials community: users upload datasets and analyze them with SOTA models and fitting methods, built as reusable FAIR-compliant modules.

research.title: Functional Genomic Representation Learning for Microbial Communities
research.hook: A parameter-free way to turn a whole microbial community into one honest vector.
research.detail: Proteogenic k-mer tokenization yields interpretable, parameter-free genome encodings that plug into both classical statistics and pretrained sequence models (Evo, ESM, ProtT5). Abundance-weighted community embeddings, leakage-safe features, permutation tests and honest cross-validation.
